source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the <image>, the open-loop transfer function of a unity gain negative feedback system is given as \( G(s) = \frac{1}{s(s+1)} \). The Nyquist contour in the s-plane encloses the entire right half-plane and a small neighborhood around the origin in the left half-plane, as shown in the <image>. The number of encirclements of the point \((-1 + j0)\) by the Nyquist plot of \( G(s) \), corresponding to the Nyquist contour, is denoted as \( N \). Then \( N \) equals ______.
extraction_notes: Removed answer-format hint prefix and kept the question body after 'Question:'.
Image field exists as a PIL image object representation, not as a path/URL, so image_paths is empty.

issues: metadata.extraction_notes state the original image field was a PIL image object representation rather than a path/URL.
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains one image path.

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'In the figure' and refers to the Nyquist contour 'as shown in the figure'.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is registered as image-reliant.
The contour geometry around the origin and enclosed half-plane is a visual/spatial element, even though part of it is also described in text.
multi_step: Solving requires combining control-theory concepts: Nyquist contour interpretation, poles of G(s)=1/(s(s+1)), and encirclement counting of (-1+j0).
The presence of a pole at the origin implies special handling of the small detour around the origin, adding at least one nontrivial reasoning step.
Answer is not directly read off from the prompt; it requires deriving the Nyquist encirclement count from the contour and transfer function.
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: '1'.
The task has a well-defined target quantity N, so a downstream checker can compare the final numeric result directly.
registry_passed=true, question and answer are present, and the image asset exists.

risk_flags: Question references a figure, so final judgment may depend on whether the image fully matches the textual contour description.
